title: Citrusy Tender Uruguayan Turnips Hash
description: A vibrant brunch hash featuring tender turnips sautéed with citrusy lemon and Argentine-inspired herbs for a bright, low-sodium morning dish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for an additional 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced turnips to the skillet, stirring to coat with oil and onions; cook for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until they begin to brown.
Sprinkle the oregano, thyme, salt, and pepper over the turnips, mixing well.
Pour in the heated vegetable broth and the zest of one lemon, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 12-15 minutes, or until the turnips are tender and the liquid has thickened slightly.
Stir in the freshly squeezed lemon juice, adjusting seasoning if necessary.
Uncover and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to evaporate excess moisture.
Transfer the hash to serving plates and sprinkle chopped parsley over the top for a fresh finish.
